title: Benefits and Services Assistance * Food Stamps/SNAP Recipients - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Food > Benefits and Services Assistance * Food Stamps/SNAP Recipients 3 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers information about applying for SNAP (food stamps) and the Oregon Health Plan, subsidized housing, counseling and support groups, and other information about local services and programs. Distributes and accepts applications for the Lions Club Sight and Hearing Program, which provides vision and hearing tests, glasses, and hearing aids to low-income individuals who have no other means of obtaining vision or hearing exams and aids. Distributes and accepts applications for dental clinics for low-income individuals without dental insurance or any realistic way of paying for dental care. 3520 Frontage Road Reedsport, OR 97467 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-2pm Email (541) 271-9700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY DCHS YOUTH AND FAMILY SERVICES DIVISION SNAP OUTREACH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Conducts community outreach and enrollment assistance to help people apply for SNAP (food stamps) and other state and local programs. 209 SW 4th Avenue Suite 200 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Income eligibility for SNAP follows a guideline of 200% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) or below. Eligibility for SNAP and other benefits is determined on a case-by-case basis. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm. Weekend and evening availability by appointment. Email (503) 823-4000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SNAP FAQ HOW TO PROTECT THE EBT CARD AND SNAP BENEFITS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF QUESTION-How can I protect my EBT card and the SNAP benefits from electronic theft? Answer: Electronic theft of benefits from Oregon EBT cards is on the rise. Skimming devices are being used to steal the card number and personal identification number (PIN). Benefits stolen electronically will not be replaced. Visit the website to protect the benefits under account services. If the member sees a charge they didn’t make, report it immediately. Cancel the card and call the replacement line. -Freeze your card between uses -Block out-of-state and online purchases -Monitor your EBT account activity Confidential Address (855) 328-6715 Visit Website More Details FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER MULTNOMAH COUNTY DCHS YOUTH AND FAMILY SERVICES DIVISION SNAP OUTREACH PROGRAM
